DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF MIRIAM SIDNEYS DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ($2,440). MOTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL APPEAR FOR A DEPOSITION ON DECEMBER 11-12, 2023. THE COURT SETS A COMPLIANCE HEARING FOR JANUARY 8, 2023 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 301 TO CONFIRM WHETHER THE DEPOSITION OCCURRED AND IF ANY SANCTIONS SHOULD BE IMPOSED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM DAVID MCDONALD AT DAVIDIDAHO14@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

COMPLIANCE HEARING TO CONFIRM WHETHER THE DEPOSITION OCCURED AND IF SANCTIONS SHOULD BE IMPOSED.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar per December 5, 2023, notice of dismissal of entire action.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Miriam Sidneys Deposition And Request For Sanctions ($2,440)
Off Calendar Dec-04-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Miriam Sidneys Deposition And Request For Sanctions ($2,440)

Motion granted. Plaintiff shall appear for a deposition on December 11-12, 2023. The Court sets a compliance hearing for January 8, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301 to confirm whether the deposition occurred and if any sanctions should be imposed.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem David McDonald at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Atty. DANA L. BURCH, Esq. of Liebert Cassidy Whitmore (Tel: 415-512-3000) for defendant State of California Public Utilities Commission;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MIRIAM SIDNEY's Motion To Compel Defendant State Of California Public Utilities Commission's Further Responses To Written Discovery; And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

The Court notes it received an email from counsel for plaintiff/moving party dated 1/2/23 requesting that this matter be taken off calendar. Counsel for defendant states that they have no objection to the matter going off calendar.

The Court takes this matter off calendar at plaintiff/moving party's request.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
